items.text: NOTICE: The Brisbane Hall elevator will be out of service from June 8 through June 12 for cable replacement. Use the north stairwell or the service elevator in Room 102. A $5 fee will be waived for late-arrival passes during this period. Contact Facilities at extension 4407.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should building users do during the closure?

options.A: Take the elevator in Brisbane Hall as usual.
options.B: Wait in the lobby for a Facilities escort.
options.C: Pay the standard $5 late-arrival pass fee.
options.D: Use the north stairwell or service elevator.

questions.explanation: The notice instructs users to "use the north stairwell or the service elevator in Room 102" during the closure period.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who arrive late during this period?

options.A: They will not be charged the late-arrival fee.
options.B: They will be denied entry to the building entirely.
options.C: They will receive a new $5 pass from Facilities.
options.D: They will need approval from the dean's office.

questions.explanation: The notice says the $5 late-arrival pass fee "will be waived" during the closure, implying late arrivals will not have to pay.

items.text: STUDENT THEATER PRESENTS: A Spring of Quiet Rooms. May 15-17, 7:30 PM, Carver Studio Theater. Tickets only $5 at the door. Free post-show talkback with the director on Saturday night. Bring student ID for entry. Doors open thirty minutes before curtain.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, when does the play take place?

options.C: On one weekend day in May.

questions.explanation: The flyer lists "May 15-17, 7:30 PM," which is three evenings.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about audience members on Saturday night?

options.A: They can speak with the director after the show.
options.B: They receive a discount on their ticket price.
options.C: They must purchase tickets at least a week early.
options.D: They have to bring their own folding chairs.

questions.explanation: The flyer mentions a "free post-show talkback with the director on Saturday night," indicating Saturday attendees can engage with the director.

items.text: Hi Daria, my office hours this week are moved. I'll be in Hayden 312 on Wednesday from 2:00 to 4:00 PM instead of the usual Friday slot. If you need to discuss your midterm draft, please drop by then or email me to reserve a 15-minute slot. Best, Professor Ahmadi.

questions.stem: According to the email, when will Professor Ahmadi hold office hours this week?

options.B: On Tuesday at the usual time.

questions.explanation: The email states the professor will be in Hayden 312 "on Wednesday from 2:00 to 4:00 PM" instead of the usual Friday slot.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want a guaranteed time?

options.A: They should arrive before the office opens.
options.B: They should bring a printed midterm draft.
options.C: They should ask the department secretary first.
options.D: They should email the professor to reserve a slot.

questions.explanation: The email tells students to "email me to reserve a 15-minute slot," suggesting reservation is the way to guarantee a time.

items.text: NOTICE: The bike racks along Linden Walk will be removed from June 14 to June 21 for sidewalk repair. Bikes still attached after June 13 will be moved to South Lot B. A $5 retrieval fee applies after that date. Contact Campus Safety at the main desk.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what will happen to bikes left after June 13?

options.A: They will be donated to a community program.
options.B: They will be returned to their owners by mail.
options.C: They will be moved to South Lot B by staff.
options.D: They will be locked in place until July 1.

questions.explanation: The notice states that bikes still attached after June 13 "will be moved to South Lot B."

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who pick up their bike on June 18?

options.A: They will not need to contact Campus Safety.
options.B: They will be charged a $5 retrieval fee.
options.C: They will be allowed to leave bikes attached.
options.D: They will receive a refund for the repair work.

questions.explanation: Picking up a bike on June 18 falls after the June 13 cutoff, and the notice says "a $5 retrieval fee applies after that date."
